app.title: Tower // Builds
progression.lead: Each run plotted over time. Hover a point for its run, tier, and build. A dashed line marks where your active build changed from the previous run — so you can see whether a reallocation moved the trend.
progression.cphNote: The tier-comparable farming KPI — coins earned per hour of run time.
progression.waveNote: Wave isn't comparable across tiers, so pick a tier to see its trend.
progression.noData: No runs with this metric yet.
progression.empty: Log a few runs (and tie each to its build) to see how your progression trends over time.
buildForm.label: Label
buildForm.editing: Editing build #{id} — updates this snapshot in place (for leveling up, not a respec).
buildForm.newSnapshotLead: A new snapshot. Or start from your last build via
buildForm.cloned: Cloned from build #{id} — change only what moved, then save as a new snapshot. Untouched fields carry forward.
buildForm.leveling: Next snapshot in this build's line (from #{id}) — everything carries forward; just bump the levels you bought. The previous snapshot is kept for the run it produced.
buildForm.shorthandHint: Enter values the way the game shows them — e.g. 869.03M, 56.4%, ×1.012, 14s. Suffix case matters: q (quadrillion) ≠ Q (quintillion).
draft.prompt: You have an unsaved draft on this form.
buildsList.caption: Saved builds
onboarding.buildsBody: Save versioned snapshots of your stats and watch your build evolve across respecs and level-ups.
onboarding.reportsBody: Paste an after-run battle report and Tower pulls out your tier, wave, coins, and time automatically — no typing.
buildDetail.noStats: This snapshot has no stored stats.
reportForm.hint: Paste your after-run battle report below. Tier, wave, coins, and duration are extracted automatically.
reportsList.caption: Logged runs
reportDetail.nextBuildQ: Spending your coins now? Capture the level-ups as your next snapshot — they apply to your next run, so recording them here means you never have to remember them later.
reportDetail.dateInferredTitle: Battle Date missing or unparseable — using insert time
error.couldntRead: Couldn't read these fields, so nothing was saved for them: {fields}. Enter values the way the game shows them — e.g. 869.03M, 56.4%, ×1.012, 14s.
error.pasteTooLarge: Paste is too large (max {kb} KB). Make sure you copied only the after-run report.
error.noReportData: Could not find battle report data in that paste — make sure you copied the full after-run report from the game.
